title: Quidditch Trials
prose: A notice goes up on the {{house}} board: trials for the house team, this Saturday. First-years, the small print reminds everyone, aren't allowed brooms of their own and essentially never make the team — but nothing stops you wandering down to the pitch to watch anyway, along with half the common room.

It's louder and faster than you expected, seven players a side wheeling and diving after balls you can barely track, the Quaffle changing hands in blurs, a Bludger cracking off a goalpost hard enough that you feel it in your teeth from the stands.
Walking back up to the castle afterward, you catch yourself already working out which position you'd want, if you ever got the chance.

choices.label: Chaser — you like the idea of being where the action is.
Keeper — someone has to be the last line of defence.
Honestly, just watching was thrilling enough.
